risk_if_not_implemented: Without Web Browser Security, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Secure Baseline Configurations
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to develop, document and maintain secure baseline configurations for Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) that are consistent with industry-accepted system hardening standards.
compensating_control_1.justification: Secure Baseline Configurations (CFG-02) provides configuration hardening that compensates for the absence of Web Browser Security (WEB-12) by eliminating unnecessary services and enforcing secure settings to reduce inherent exposure. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Endpoint Protection Measures
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability and safety of endpoint devices.
compensating_control_2.justification: Endpoint Protection Measures (END-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Web Browser Security (WEB-12) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
